name: Auto-Archive Lifecycle

tasks.title: Audit
tasks.description: Verify all work in Auto-Archive Lifecycle is complete and correct

scope: Implement auto-archiving for completed top-level orchestrators. Fully complete orchestrators (all children, all audits) auto-archive after a configurable delay (default: 24h since last activity). Archived nodes move from system/projects/ to archive/. Status shows active work plus a count line ('N archived nodes'). New flags: status --archived (show only archived), status --all (show everything). New commands: wolfcastle archive add --node <addr>, wolfcastle archive restore --node <addr>, wolfcastle archive delete --node <addr>. Needs: archive state tracking (completion timestamp per node), archive move logic, status rendering changes, CLI commands, config for delay threshold, daemon auto-archive timer. Also requires test coverage above 90% for all new and modified code — particularly archive move/restore/delete logic, status rendering with archive flags, and the daemon auto-archive timer. Run go test -coverprofile and verify no package dropped below its pre-change coverage.
success_criteria: wolfcastle archive add moves completed node state from system/projects/ to archive storage and generates markdown rollup
wolfcastle archive restore returns an archived node to the active project tree
wolfcastle archive delete permanently removes archived node state
Daemon auto-archives completed top-level orchestrators after configurable delay (default 24h)
wolfcastle status shows only active nodes by default with an 'N archived nodes' count line
wolfcastle status --archived shows only archived nodes
wolfcastle status --all shows both active and archived nodes
All new and modified code maintains >90% test coverage

breadcrumbs.text: Created 4 children: archive-service-spec (leaf, spec first), archive-implementation (orchestrator, core logic), archive-status-rendering (leaf, status command changes), daemon-auto-archive (leaf, daemon timer). Ordering: spec defines the contract before any implementation begins; archive-implementation groups config, state types, service logic, and CLI commands under a sub-orchestrator for its own decomposition; status rendering depends on the archive service being available; daemon timer is last because it depends on both the service and config.
